title: Presidential Alert
alt: When putting his kids to bed, after saying 'Goodnight', Obama has to stop himself from saying 'God bless you, and God bless the United States of America.'
transcript:
[Television beeping.]
Screen reads: "E.A.S Incoming Presidential Alert"

[President of the United States of America in his office is on the television.]
My fellow Americans. I, uhhh. Wow.

Frankly, I didn't realize what this button did. I was just... I mean...
I appear before you tonight to, um.

Look, uhh...
Remember to floss regularly. Oral hygiene is important. Thank you.
explanation:
The  allows the U.S. President to address the country in the event of a national emergency, by broadcasting a message over all television and radio channels. Despite systems like this having existed for over 60 years, no president has ever used it, even during major incidents like the . In this comic, the US President accidentally activates the system by pressing a button, apparently located on the  in the . Surprised by being on television, he tries to think of something important to say on the spot, but cannot think of anything other than a piece of generic dental-hygiene advice – a rather non-urgent message.

The concept of the President mistakenly hitting an important button has long been a source for jokes, often somewhat morbidly involving the .

The title text references the typical conclusion to presidential speeches: "Goodnight, God bless you, and God bless the United States of America," or some variation thereof. As "goodnight" is the typical conclusion to a day, the title text jokes that , out of habit, has a hard time stopping at goodnight when saying that to his children. It might also be implying that the president that sent the message might actually be Barack Obama.
